intro:
Tomorrow we are closed.
You can request repeat prescriptions for chronic medication 24 hours a day (also on weekends) in 2 ways:
patientenomgeving: You can request repeat prescriptions online via the patient environment on the website. You will need to register first. Please note that after creating an account, it can take up to a maximum of 7 days before the account is active.
receptenlijn: Call your own GP's practice telephone number and select the option for the prescription line.
receptenlijnVragen: Name and date of birth of the person for whom the prescription is intended
Street and house number of residence address
Name or names of the medications you are ordering (have the boxes at hand!)
welkeMedicijnen:
You can only request prescriptions for medications that you use on the advice of your GP or specialist chronically (usually daily).

If you wish to request medications for newly occurring complaints or temporary medications -- which you have been given for a certain period -- you cannot order these medicines not via the prescription line or website. For this you can call the practice assistant during office hours, if necessary your GP will be consulted about the request.
wanneerKlaar: If you leave a message on the prescription line or order a prescription via the website before 12:00 noon, you can collect your medications from your own pharmacy 2 working days later (from 15:00 they will be ready).
wanneerKlaarVoorbeelden: If you call/email on Tuesday evening, your medications will be ready at the pharmacy on Friday.
If you call/email on Friday afternoon after 12:00 or during the weekend, you can collect your medications on Wednesday after 15:00.
